layout_description:
Option: OPT-1-REVISED
Slide description: TWO-COLUMN MARVIN TABLE LAYOUT with full-width title at top and full-width footer text at bottom. The slide uses a classic Marvin table structure (Section 4, Option A) to organize the two service offerings side by side as equal-weight columns. The title spans the full slide width at the top with a visible gap below it. The two content columns (Technology Platform and Capacity Building) are treated as equal-peer columns in a Marvin table, each containing a column header with underline, an icon/badge ornament, a subtitle, and a bullet list of capabilities. Columns are separated by a small gutter for visual breathing room. Dashed grey separator lines between bullet items create visual rhythm without consuming vertical space. The footer text sits at the bottom as a full-width text block with a visible top margin. The layout follows a top-to-bottom reading flow: title → table columns → footer. All elements snap to a 4-column grid with consistent margins and gutters. The design applies brand colors by role name only, uses dashed separators extensively per Section 0.10, and ensures content-aware spacing per Section 0.1. CRITICAL REVISION: Body font size in both columns increased to large (20–22pt range) to fill at least 65% of container inner height, resolving the severe under-fill issue identified in judge feedback. Subtitle font size increased proportionally to medium-large (18pt range). Vertical spacing between bullet items expanded to distribute content evenly across full container height. All font size increases applied identically to both columns to preserve horizontal row alignment (Rule C6, Rule B2).

Blueprint:
  Orientation: left-to-right
  Regions: 4
  Allocation: [0.12, 0.4, 0.4, 0.08]

Containers:
  C0 (LEFT COLUMN — Technology Platform): This container is the left column of a two-column Marvin table (Section 4, Option A). It occupies the left half of the slide body area, with equal width to the right column (split 1:1). The column structure from top to bottom: (1) Icon ornament — a small circular badge with a laptop/computer icon in brand accent color, positioned at the top-left of the column, sized proportionally to the header text height (small, jewel-like detail per Rule 0.4). (2) Column header — 'TECHNOLOGY PLATFORM' in bold, brand primary color, left-aligned, full column width. Header font size remains unchanged from previous attempt (appropriate scale). (3) Header underline — a solid horizontal line in brand dark color, full column width, thin stroke, placed directly beneath the header text with no gap. (4) Subtitle — 'Cryptocurrency Analytics Capabilities' in italic body font, brand text color, left-aligned, with a small top margin below the underline. REVISED: Subtitle font size increased to medium-large (approximately 18pt) to maintain proportional hierarchy with the enlarged bullet text below. (5) Bullet list — six bullet items describing platform capabilities. Each bullet uses a small brand-color circle as the bullet marker (not native PowerPoint bullets), sized proportionally to the text height. REVISED: Bullet text font size increased to large (20–22pt range) to fill at least 65% of the container's inner vertical space, addressing the critical under-fill violation from judge feedback. Bullet text is body font, brand text color, left-aligned with a small left inset. Bullets are distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space using equal flexGrow on each bullet row section. REVISED: Vertical spacing between bullet rows expanded significantly to distribute content across the full container height — no large gap at bottom. (6) Dashed grey separator lines — thin horizontal dashed lines in light grey, full column width, placed between each consecutive bullet item (not after the last bullet). With the increased font sizes and expanded spacing, the separators now distribute naturally across the full container height, creating visual rhythm per Rule 0.10. The column has a small internal padding on all sides. No background fill, no border on the column itself. Content now fills the available vertical space effectively, with bullet text and spacing occupying at least 65% of inner height.
  C1 (RIGHT COLUMN — Capacity Building): This container is the right column of a two-column Marvin table (Section 4, Option A). It occupies the right half of the slide body area, with equal width to the left column (split 1:1). The column structure from top to bottom: (1) Icon ornament — a small circular badge with a graduation cap icon in brand accent color, positioned at the top-left of the column, sized proportionally to the header text height, matching the exact size and style of the left column icon (small, jewel-like detail per Rule 0.4). (2) Column header — 'CAPACITY BUILDING' in bold, brand primary color, left-aligned, full column width. Header font size remains unchanged from previous attempt (appropriate scale). (3) Header underline — a solid horizontal line in brand dark color, full column width, thin stroke, placed directly beneath the header text with no gap. (4) Subtitle — 'Training & Knowledge Transfer' in italic body font, brand text color, left-aligned, with a small top margin below the underline. REVISED: Subtitle font size increased to medium-large (approximately 18pt), IDENTICAL to the left column subtitle, to maintain proportional hierarchy with the enlarged bullet text below and preserve within-slide uniformity (Rule C6). (5) Bullet list — six bullet items describing training offerings. Each bullet uses a small brand-color circle as the bullet marker (not native PowerPoint bullets), sized proportionally to the text height. REVISED: Bullet text font size increased to large (20–22pt range), IDENTICAL to the left column bullet text, to fill at least 65% of the container's inner vertical space and resolve the critical under-fill violation. Bullet text is body font, brand text color, left-aligned with a small left inset. Bullets are distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space using equal flexGrow on each bullet row section, with flexGrow values IDENTICAL to the left column so that row boundaries align horizontally across both columns (Rule B2 — horizontal alignment of row sections is mandatory for Marvin tables). REVISED: Vertical spacing between bullet rows expanded significantly, IDENTICAL to the left column spacing, to distribute content across the full container height — no large gap at bottom. (6) Dashed grey separator lines — thin horizontal dashed lines in light grey, full column width, placed between each consecutive bullet item (not after the last bullet). The separators must be positioned at EXACTLY the same vertical positions as the separators in the left column to enforce horizontal alignment (Rule B2). With the increased font sizes and expanded spacing applied identically to both columns, the separators now distribute naturally across the full container height in both columns, maintaining perfect horizontal alignment and creating visual rhythm per Rule 0.10. The column has a small internal padding on all sides, IDENTICAL to the left column. No background fill, no border on the column itself. Content now fills the available vertical space effectively, with bullet text and spacing occupying at least 65% of inner height, matching the left column fill ratio exactly.
  C2 (FOOTER TEXT — Partnership Statement): This container holds the footer text that sits at the bottom of the slide, below the two-column table. It spans the full slide width (same left and right margins as the title). The text reads: 'Log-term partnerships including support training and ability to adapt and provide solutions to emerging needs' (note: the source text contains 'Log-term' which appears to be a typo for 'Long-term', but is preserved as-is per content fidelity rules unless explicit correction is requested). The text is rendered in body font, brand text color, left-aligned, with a visible top margin separating it from the bottom edge of the table columns above. Font size is standard body size (unchanged from previous attempt — this container was not flagged in judge feedback). No background fill, no border, no decorative elements. The text wraps naturally to 2–3 lines depending on final font size and slide width, with a small bottom margin from the slide edge. This is a plain text block, not a colored footer bar. The container height is compact — just enough to contain the text with minimal padding above and below.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER — Inactive): The bottom decorative layer is NOT ACTIVE for this slide. The two-column Marvin table structure fills the slide body area effectively, and with the revised font sizes and spacing, the content density now fills at least 65% of each column's inner height. The columns are visually unified by their shared structure (matching headers, underlines, bullet styles, and aligned separator lines) without requiring a background card or panel. Per Rule D1, the decorative layer is used only when content would leave the slide looking empty or when visual grouping cannot be achieved through container design alone — neither condition applies here. The revised layout resolves the under-fill issue through increased font sizes and expanded spacing, not through addition of a decorative background element.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER — Inactive): The top connector layer is NOT ACTIVE for this slide. There are no cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements required. The two columns are independent content sections with no visual links between them. Per Rule E3, the top layer is used only for elements that genuinely span containers or sit above all containers — no such elements exist in this layout.
